rxn_class_summary: Electrocatalytic cyclopropanation of arylidene- or alkylidenemalononitriles with dialkyl malonates in alcoholic NaBr/NaI to give 3-substituted 2,2-dicyanocyclopropane-1,1-dicarboxylates.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide (Br- or I-) to molecular halogen (Hal2) which serves as the chemical mediator; cathodic reduction of alcohol generates alkoxide base that deprotonates the malonate CH-acid. Substrate itself is not directly oxidized at the electrode.

key_intermediates.name: molecular halogen Hal2 (Br2 or I2) generated at anode
alkoxide ion (MeO- or EtO-) generated at cathode
intermediate anion B from malononitrile + arylidenemalonate (Scheme 7)
cyclic tetraester 6 (side product from malonate + arylidenemalonate)
dimethyl 3,3-dicyano-2-phenylpropane-1,1-dicarboxylate (4, open-chain Michael adduct detected at low charge)

electron_flow: At the anode, halide is oxidized to Hal2 (2 Hal- - 2e -> Hal2); at the cathode, alcohol is reduced to alkoxide and H2. Alkoxide deprotonates dialkyl malonate to its carbanion, which undergoes Michael addition to the arylidene/alkylidenemalononitrile to give anion A; A is halogenated by Hal2 and then closes the cyclopropane ring via intramolecular SN2 with displacement of Hal- on action of alkoxide.
rate_determining_step: Deprotonation of the CH-acid and/or Michael addition of the malonate anion to the activated olefin (NaBr preferred over NaI because bromomalonate is a stronger CH-acid and adds faster).

probe_reagents.name: reduced charge (0.7 F mol-1) electrolysis
probe_reagents.observation: Open-chain Michael adduct 4 (dimethyl 3,3-dicyano-2-phenylpropane-1,1-dicarboxylate) detected by 1H NMR in 25% along with cyclopropane 3a (32%), supporting stepwise Michael then halogenation/ring-closure.

probe_reagents.name: NaI vs NaBr mediator comparison
probe_reagents.observation: NaBr gives higher yields than NaI for malonate substrates (e.g. 75 vs 63%), opposite to tetracarboxylate ring closure; attributed to faster bromomalonate formation/deprotonation and faster Michael addition.

probe_reagents.name: temperature variation (-20 to +20 C)
probe_reagents.observation: 0 C optimal for malonate + alkylidenemalononitrile; raising T causes oligomerization and lower yields.

notes: An alternative/parallel route (Scheme 5) is proposed in which malonate is first brominated at the anode to bromomalonate, deprotonated to bromomalonate anion, then adds to the activated olefin followed by base-induced ring closure; this rationalizes why NaBr outperforms NaI here (opposite trend to the tetracarboxylate cyclization, ref 18). A side pathway via malononitrile-anion route (Schemes 6-8) gives lower yields owing to oligomerization of malononitrile and formation of cyclic tetraester 6 from CH(COOMe)2- + arylidenemalonate.
